content: Steve handles engineering and infrastructure.
Sadie owns marketing content and social scheduling.
Kiera is responsible for QA and release verification.
Boss is the CEO orchestrator who delegates top-level tasks.
The task-board coordinates work across four agents: boss, steve, sadie, kiera.
Snoopy runs the L0 superuser session with full MCP access.
Coach Stokes owns final approval on all financial commitments.
GweiSprayer is the primary Telegram contact for status updates.
The runner sub-agent handles simple bounded tasks; the agent sub-agent handles complex multi-step work.
Two8 Bands is the main Shopify storefront the team supports.
Prefer WebP images for product pages over JPEG or PNG.
Always use UTC timestamps when logging task events.
Default to bun test over npm test for this repository.
Keep commit messages under 70 characters for the title line.
Prefer Firecrawl over WebFetch for any webpage content extraction.
Use ISO 8601 date formatting in all memory entries.
Favor ephemeral SQLite DBs under /tmp for test isolation.
Prefer delegating complex work to the opus sub-agent, not the main thread.
Default new feature flags to OFF until a deploy gate passes.
Prefer additive schema migrations over destructive ALTER TABLE drops.
Shopify API returns UTC timestamps for all order records.
The task-board SQLite file lives under ~/.claude/mcp-servers/task-board.
Amazon Associates tag for the two8 blog is stephenstokes-20.
Apollo's free tier covers person match and enrich but gates the LinkedIn URL field.
Netlify environment variables are scoped per site, not per team.
The heartbeat daemon polls every five minutes via launchd.
GA4 is not wired up for two8.shop web analytics yet.
feature_flags table stores flag_name and enabled columns.
The circuit breaker opens after repeated faults on an agent session.
Watchdog lease table enforces a single active lease row via a CHECK constraint.
Delegation briefs are capped at eight memories and eight thousand bytes.
The recall path blends BM25 and dense vector scores with configured weights.
Consolidation runs periodically to merge near-duplicate memories.
Debrief demotes memory importance by a factor of 0.3 by default.
Superseded memories retain a supersedes_memory_id pointer to their predecessor.
Completed the Q3 ads audit; CTR up 12% quarter over quarter.
Finished migrating the delegation-brief schema; all existing tests still pass.
Closed out the Smartlead warm-up verification with a confirmed pass.
Wrapped up the Father's Day promo bundle-exclusion config fix.
Shipped the P3 curated delegation briefs behind a default-off flag.
Reconciled the Threadwork backup push and rotated two exposed tokens.
Resolved the circuit-breaker spam by resetting the agent_sessions row.
Verified the two8 GSC keyword harvest edge function end to end.
Audited RLS policies across all Supabase tables with no gaps found.
Ran the memory health report and archived twelve stale entries.
Deployed the dashboard update to Coachstokes/threadwork-dashboard.
Completed the Apollo enrichment integration for pre-call briefs.
Finished the state-contracts migration and backfilled state_changed_at.
Closed the loop on the watchdog lease table hardening.
Validated the consolidator's dedup threshold against the eval corpus.
A build sub-agent's own secrets-scan can produce false negatives; verify independently.
Compaction itself is a drift step; avoid relying on a second compaction in one session.
Objective grading requires publishing the exact regex weights and formula.
Hard external deadlines need a scheduled wakeup, not just message-driven nudges.
Browser-based key acquisition works well against an already-signed-in console.
The instructions in the runbook are up to date.
System configuration lives in config.ts and loads defaults at boot.
The user interface refreshes every thirty seconds.
Boss directive emails go out every Monday morning.
Session handoff notes should be reviewed each morning.
